title: Microstylis resupinata
intro: Microstylis resupinata, also known as the Indian cobra lily, is a type of orchid found in the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in treating various health conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. Microstylis resupinata contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, as well as to support overall well-being.
taxonomy: Microstylis resupinata is a species of orchid. It is classified in the genus Microstylis, which is part of the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The family Orchidaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, commonly known as orchids. Microstylis resupinata is a terrestrial orchid native to tropical Asia. Further classification details may include its order (Asparagales) and kingdom (Plantae).

parts: Microstylis resupinata, also known as the Resupinate Orchid, is a plant species found in various parts of the world.
